Option_C: Use AWS Lambda with a new code that uses one of the supported languages. Create multiple Lambda functions to support the load. Use Amazon API Gateway as an entry point to the Lambda functions.
Option_D: Use a high performance computing (HPC) solution such as AWS ParallelCluster to establish an HPC cluster that can process the incoming requests at the appropriate scale.
Topics: AWS Fargate, Amazon ECS, Application Load Balancer

Question:
A company has 20 TB of existing data that needs to be moved to an Amazon S3 bucket. The company’s data center does not have any available network bandwidth for additional workloads. A solutions architect must transfer the data and must configure the transformation job to continue to run in the AWS Cloud.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
Option_A: Use AWS DataSync to move the data. Create a custom transformation job by using AWS Glue.
Option_B: Order an AWS Snowcone device to move the data. Deploy the transformation application to the device.
Option_C: Order an AWS Snowball Edge Storage Optimized device. Copy the data to the device. Create a custom transformation job by using AWS Glue.
Option_D: Order an AWS Snowball Edge Storage Optimized device that includes Amazon EC2 compute. Copy the data to the device. Create a new EC2 instance on AWS to run the transformation application.

Question:
A company has created an image analysis application in which users can upload photos and add photo frames to their images. The users upload images and metadata to indicate which photo frames they want to add to their images. The application uses a single Amazon EC2 instance and Amazon DynamoDB to store the metadata.
The application is becoming more popular, and the number of users is increasing. The company expects the number of concurrent users to vary significantly depending on the time of day and day of week. The company must ensure that the application can scale to meet demand with the LEAST operational overhead.
Which combination of actions will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
Option_A: Migrate the application to run on an Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling group behind an Application Load Balancer. Use Amazon S3 to store the photos.
Option_B: Migrate the application's image processing from the EC2 instance to an AWS Lambda function that processes the images.
Option_C: Migrate the application's web interface to a static website that is hosted in an Amazon S3 bucket.
Option_D: Migrate the application's photo storage from the EC2 instance to an Amazon S3 bucket.
Option_E: Migrate the application's metadata storage from Amazon DynamoDB to Amazon Aurora.
Topics: Amazon S3, AWS Lambda, Amazon DynamoDB

Question:
A company wants to migrate an on-premises data center to AWS. The data center hosts an SFTP server that stores its data on an NFS-based file system. The server holds 200 GB of data that needs to be transferred. The server must be hosted on an Amazon EC2 instance that uses an Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) file system.
Which combination of steps should a solutions architect take to automate this task? (Choose two.)
Option_A: Launch the EC2 instance into the same Availability Zone as the EFS file system.
Option_B: Install an AWS DataSync agent in the on-premises data center.
Option_C: Create a secondary Amazon EFS file system in a different Availability Zone.
Option_D: Manually use an operating system copy command to push the data to the EC2 instance.
Option_E: Configure an AWS DataSync task to transfer the data from the on-premises NFS server to the EFS file system.

Question:
A company has an AWS Glue extract, transform, and load (ETL) job that runs every day at the same time. The job processes XML data that is in an Amazon S3 bucket. New data is added to the S3 bucket every day. A solutions architect notices that AWS Glue is processing all the data during each run.
What should the solutions architect do to prevent AWS Glue from reprocessing old data?
Option_A: Edit the job to use job bookmarks.
Option_B: Edit the job to delete data after the data is processed.
Option_C: Edit the job by setting the NumberOfWorkers field to 1.
Option_D: Use a FindMatches machine learning (ML) transform.

Question:
A solutions architect must design a highly available infrastructure for a website. The website is powered by Windows web servers that run on Amazon EC2 instances. The solutions architect must implement a solution that can mitigate a large-scale DDoS attack that originates from thousands of IP addresses. Downtime is not acceptable for the website.
Which actions should the solutions architect take to protect the website from such an attack? (Choose two.)
Option_A: Use AWS Shield Advanced to stop the DDoS attack.
Option_B: Configure Amazon GuardDuty to automatically block the attackers.
Option_C: Configure the website to use Amazon CloudFront for both static and dynamic content.
Option_D: Use an AWS Lambda function to automatically add attacker IP addresses to VPC network ACLs.
Option_E: Use EC2 Spot Instances in an Auto Scaling group with a target tracking scaling policy that is set to 80% CPU utilization.

Question:
A company is preparing to deploy a new serverless workload. A solutions architect must use the principle of least privilege to configure permissions that will be used to run an AWS Lambda function. An Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) rule will invoke the function.
Which solution meets these requirements?
Option_A: Add an execution role to the function with lambda:InvokeFunction as the action and * as the principal.
Option_B: Add an execution role to the function with lambda:InvokeFunction as the action and Service: lambda.amazonaws.com as the principal.
Option_C: Add a resource-based policy to the function with lambda:* as the action and Service: events.amazonaws.com as the principal.
Option_D: Add a resource-based policy to the function with lambda:InvokeFunction as the action and Service: events.amazonaws.com as the principal.

Question:
A company stores confidential data in an Amazon S3 bucket. All data must be encrypted at rest, and key usage must be logged for auditing. The company also requires that the encryption key be rotated every year.
Which solution meets these requirements and is the MOST operationally efficient?
Option_A: Server-side encryption with customer-provided keys (SSE-C)
Option_B: Server-side encryption with Amazon S3 managed keys (SSE-S3)
Option_C: Server-side encryption with AWS KMS keys (SSE-KMS) with manual rotation
Option_D: Server-side encryption with AWS KMS keys (SSE-KMS) with automatic rotation

Question:
A company is building an application to track the real-time location of bicycles. The company needs to make this location data available through a REST API to an existing analytics platform.
Which combination of AWS services will meet these requirements?
Option_A: Amazon Athena and Amazon S3
Option_B: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams and Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose
Option_C: Amazon API Gateway and AWS Lambda
Option_D: Amazon DynamoDB and Amazon S3 Glacier

Question:
A car sales company needs to build a new system that publishes real-time events when an automobile is sold. Several target systems need to consume these events, and each target system has different requirements regarding how it consumes the events.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
Option_A: Publish RDS event notifications to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) Topics with multiple Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) subscriptions. Configure a single AWS Lambda function to filter the events and forward the events to the correct target system.
Option_B: Use RDS event notifications to publish messages to an Amazon Kinesis data stream. Filter the messages by using Amazon Kinesis Data Analytics. Configure the target systems to subscribe to the Kinesis data stream.
Option_C: Write the automobile sales data to Amazon DynamoDB. Use DynamoDB Streams to publish the events. Filter the events by using an AWS Lambda function. Configure the target systems to subscribe to the DynamoDB stream.
Option_D: Use RDS event notifications. Send the notifications to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) Topics. Fan out the messages to multiple Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queues. Configure AWS Lambda functions to consume messages from the SQS queues.
Topics: Amazon RDS, Amazon SNS, Amazon SQS, AWS Lambda

Question:
A company wants to manage a repository of documentation for its development team. The documents must be protected from accidental or malicious modification. The company wants to make sure that the original documents remain unmodified. However, users must be able to modify the objects in a new version of the objects. The company wants a solution that requires minimum operational overhead.
Which solution will meet these requirements?
Option_A: Create an S3 bucket and use versioning. Replicate data to a different AWS Region. Update the S3 bucket policy to deny the s3:DeleteObject action to all users.
Option_B: Create an S3 bucket. Enable S3 Event Notifications to send a message to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) Topics when objects are modified or deleted. Configure an AWS Lambda function to revert changes.
Option_C: Create an S3 bucket. Use AWS CloudTrail to track any S3 API events that modify the objects. Upon notification, restore the modified objects from any backup versions that the company has.
Option_D: Create an S3 bucket with S3 Object Lock enabled. Enable versioning. Add a legal hold to the objects. Add the s3:PutObjectLegalHold permission to the IAM policies of users who need to delete the objects.

Question:
A company hosts an ecommerce website on Amazon EC2. During upload requests, the website resizes the images to a standard size and stores the resized images in Amazon S3. Users are experiencing slow upload requests to the website.
The company needs to reduce coupling within the application and improve website performance. A solutions architect must design the most operationally efficient process for image uploads.
Which combination of actions should the solutions architect take to meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
Option_A: Configure the application to upload images to S3 Glacier.
Option_B: Configure the web server to upload the original images to Amazon S3.
Option_C: Configure the application to upload images directly from each user's browser to Amazon S3 through the use of a presigned URL
Option_D: Configure S3 Event Notifications to invoke an AWS Lambda function when an image is uploaded. Use the function to resize the image.
Option_E: Create an Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) rule that invokes an AWS Lambda function on a schedule to resize uploaded images.

Question:
A company wants to migrate an existing containerized web application to AWS. The company needs to minimize code changes, minimize development effort, and minimize operational overhead. The web application runs on containers, has dynamic scaling, and requires an Application Load Balancer to distribute incoming traffic. The application needs to be resilient to instance failures.
Which solution meets these requirements?
Option_A: Use AWS Fargate on Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) to run the containerized web application with Service Auto Scaling. Use an Application Load Balancer to distribute the incoming requests.
Option_B: Use two Amazon EC2 instances to host the containerized web application. Use an Application Load Balancer to distribute the incoming requests.
Option_C: Use AWS Lambda with a new code that uses one of the supported languages. Create multiple Lambda functions to support the load. Use Amazon API Gateway as an entry point to the Lambda functions.
Option_D: Use a high performance computing (HPC) solution such as AWS ParallelCluster to establish an HPC cluster that can process the incoming requests at the appropriate scale.
Topics: AWS Fargate, Amazon ECS, Application Load Balancer

Option_B: Order an AWS Snowcone device to move the data. Deploy the transformation application to the device.

Question:
A company recently launched a variety of new workloads on Amazon EC2 instances in its AWS account. The company needs to create a strategy to access and administer the instances remotely and securely. The company needs to implement a repeatable process that works with native AWS services and follows the AWS Well-Architected Framework.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
Option_A: Use the EC2 serial console to directly access the terminal interface of each instance for administration.
Option_B: Attach the appropriate IAM role to each existing instance and new instance. Use AWS Systems Manager Session Manager to establish a remote SSH session.
Option_C: Create an administrative SSH key pair. Load the public key into each EC2 instance. Deploy a bastion host in a public subnet to provide a tunnel for administration of each instance.
Option_D: Establish an AWS Site-to-Site VPN connection. Instruct administrators to use their local on-premises machines to connect directly to the instances by using SSH keys across the VPN tunnel.

Question:
A company is hosting a static website on Amazon S3 and is using Amazon Route 53 for DNS. The website is experiencing increased demand from around the world. The company must decrease latency for users who access the website.
Which solution meets these requirements MOST cost-effectively?
Option_A: Replicate the S3 bucket that contains the website to all AWS Regions. Add Route 53 geolocation routing entries.
Option_B: Provision accelerators in AWS Global Accelerator. Associate the supplied IP addresses with the S3 bucket. Edit the Route 53 entries to point to the IP addresses of the accelerators.
Option_C: Add an Amazon CloudFront distribution in front of the S3 bucket. Edit the Route 53 entries to point to the CloudFront distribution.
Option_D: Enable S3 Transfer Acceleration on the bucket. Edit the Route 53 entries to point to the new endpoint.

Question:
A company maintains a searchable repository of items on its website. The data is stored in an Amazon RDS for MySQL database table that contains more than 10 million rows. The database has 2 TB of General Purpose SSD storage. There are millions of updates against this data every day through the company's website.
The company has noticed that some insert operations are taking 10 seconds or longer. The company has determined that the database storage performance is the problem.
Which solution addresses this performance issue?
Option_A: Change the storage type to Provisioned IOPS SSD.
Option_B: Change the DB instance to a memory optimized instance class.
Option_C: Change the DB instance to a burstable performance instance class.
Option_D: Enable Multi-AZ RDS read replicas with MySQL native asynchronous replication.

Question: A company has thousands of edge devices that collectively generate 1 TB of status alerts each day. Each alert is approximately 2 KB in size. A solutions architect needs to implement a solution to ingest and store the alerts for future analysis. The company wants a highly available solution. However, the company needs to minimize costs and does not want to manage additional infrastructure. Additionally, the company wants to keep 14 days of data available for immediate analysis and archive any data older than 14 days. What is the MOST operationally efficient solution that meets these requirements?
Option_A: Create an Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose delivery stream to ingest the alerts. Configure the Kinesis Data Firehose stream to deliver the alerts to an Amazon S3 bucket. Set up an S3 Lifecycle configuration to transition data to Amazon S3 Glacier after 14 days.
Option_B: Launch Amazon EC2 instances across two Availability Zones and place them behind an Elastic Load Balancer to ingest the alerts. Create a script on the EC2 instances that will store the alerts in an Amazon S3 bucket. Set up an S3 Lifecycle configuration to transition data to Amazon S3 Glacier after 14 days.
Option_C: Create an Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose delivery stream to ingest the alerts. Configure the Kinesis Data Firehose stream to deliver the alerts to an Amazon OpenSearch Service (Amazon Elasticsearch Service) cluster. Set up the Amazon OpenSearch Service (Amazon Elasticsearch Service) cluster to take manual snapshots every day and delete data from the cluster that is older than 14 days.
Option_D: Create an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) standard queue to ingest the alerts, and set the message retention period to 14 days. Configure consumers to poll the SQS queue, check the age of the message, and analyze the message data as needed. If the message is 14 days old, the consumer should copy the message to an Amazon S3 bucket and delete the message from the SQS queue.

Question: A company's application integrates with multiple software-as-a-service (SaaS) sources for data collection. The company runs Amazon EC2 instances to receive the data and to upload the data to an Amazon S3 bucket for analysis. The same EC2 instance that receives and uploads the data also sends a notification to the user when an upload is complete. The company has noticed slow application performance and wants to improve the performance as much as possible. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
Option_A: Create an Auto Scaling group so that EC2 instances can scale out. Configure an S3 event notification to send events to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) Topics when the upload to the S3 bucket is complete.
Option_B: Create an Amazon AppFlow flow to transfer data between each SaaS source and the S3 bucket. Configure an S3 event notification to send events to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) Topics when the upload to the S3 bucket is complete.
Option_C: Create an Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) rule for each SaaS source to send output data. Configure the S3 bucket as the rule's target. Create a second EventBridge (Cloud Watch Events) rule to send events when the upload to the S3 bucket is complete. Configure an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) Topics as the second rule's target.
Option_D: Create a Docker container to use instead of an EC2 instance. Host the containerized application on Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS). Configure Amazon CloudWatch Container Insights to send events to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) Topics when the upload to the S3 bucket is complete.

Question: A company runs a highly available image-processing application on Amazon EC2 instances in a single VPC. The EC2 instances run inside several subnets across multiple Availability Zones. The EC2 instances do not communicate with each other. However, the EC2 instances download images from Amazon S3 and upload images to Amazon S3 through a single NAT gateway. The company is concerned about data transfer charges. What is the MOST cost-effective way for the company to avoid Regional data transfer charges?
Option_A: Launch the NAT gateway in each Availability Zone.
Option_B: Replace the NAT gateway with a NAT instance.
Option_C: Deploy a gateway VPC endpoint for Amazon S3.
Option_D: Provision an EC2 Dedicated Host to run the EC2 instances.

Question: A company has an on-premises application that generates a large amount of time-sensitive data that is backed up to Amazon S3. The application has grown and there are user complaints about internet bandwidth limitations. A solutions architect needs to design a long-term solution that allows for both timely backups to Amazon S3 and with minimal impact on internet connectivity for internal users. Which solution meets these requirements?
Option_A: Establish AWS VPN connections and proxy all traffic through a VPC gateway endpoint.
Option_B: Establish a new AWS Direct Connect connection and direct backup traffic through this new connection.
Option_C: Order daily AWS Snowball devices. Load the data onto the Snowball devices and return the devices to AWS each day.
Option_D: Submit a support ticket through the AWS Management Console. Request the removal of S3 service limits from the account.

Question: A company has an Amazon S3 bucket that contains critical data. The company must protect the data from accidental deletion. Which combination of steps should a solutions architect take to meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
Option_A: Enable versioning on the S3 bucket.
Option_B: Enable MFA Delete on the S3 bucket.
Option_C: Create a bucket policy on the S3 bucket.
Option_D: Enable default encryption on the S3 bucket.
Option_E: Create a lifecycle policy for the objects in the S3 bucket.

Question: A company has a data ingestion workflow that consists of the following: An Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) Topics for notifications about new data deliveries; An AWS Lambda function to process the data and record metadata. The company observes that the ingestion workflow fails occasionally because of network connectivity issues. When such a failure occurs, the Lambda function does not ingest the corresponding data unless the company manually reruns the job. Which combination of actions should a solutions architect take to ensure that the Lambda function ingests all data in the future? (Choose two.)
Option_A: Deploy the Lambda function in multiple Availability Zones.
Option_B: Create an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue, and subscribe it to the SNS Topics.
Option_C: Increase the CPU and memory that are allocated to the Lambda function.
Option_D: Increase provisioned throughput for the Lambda function.
Option_E: Modify the Lambda function to read from an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue.

Question: A company has an application that provides marketing services to stores. The services are based on previous purchases by store customers. The stores upload transaction data to the company through SFTP, and the data is processed and analyzed to generate new marketing offers. Some of the files can exceed 200 GB in size. Recently, the company discovered that some of the stores have uploaded files that contain personally identifiable information (PII) that should not have been included. The company wants administrators to be alerted if Pll is shared again. The company also wants to automate remediation. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements with the LEAST development effort?
Option_A: Use an Amazon S3 bucket as a secure transfer point. Use Amazon Inspector to scan the objects in the bucket. If objects contain Pll, trigger an S3 Lifecycle policy to remove the objects that contain PII.
Option_B: Use an Amazon S3 bucket as a secure transfer point. Use Amazon Macie to scan the objects in the bucket. If objects contain PII, use Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) to trigger a notification to the administrators to remove the objects that contain PII.
Option_C: Implement custom scanning algorithms in an AWS Lambda function. Trigger the function when objects are loaded into the bucket. If objects contain PII, use Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) to trigger a notification to the administrators to remove the objects that contain PII.
Option_D: Implement custom scanning algorithms in an AWS Lambda function. Trigger the function when objects are loaded into the bucket. If objects contain Pll, use Amazon Simple Email Service (Amazon SES) to trigger a notification to the administrators.

Question: A company needs guaranteed Amazon EC2 capacity in three specific Availability Zones in a specific AWS Region for an upcoming event that will last 1 week. What should the company do to guarantee the EC2 capacity?
Option_A: Purchase Reserved Instances that specify the Region needed.
Option_B: Create an On-Demand Capacity Reservation that specifies the Region needed.
Option_C: Purchase Reserved Instances that specify the Region and three Availability Zones needed.
Option_D: Create an On-Demand Capacity Reservation that specifies the Region and three Availability Zones needed.

Question: A company's website uses an Amazon EC2 instance store for its catalog of items. The company wants to make sure that the catalog is highly available and that the catalog is stored in a durable location. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?
Option_A: Move the catalog to Amazon ElastiCache for Redis.
Option_B: Deploy a larger EC2 instance with a larger instance store.
Option_C: Move the catalog from the instance store to Amazon S3 Glacier Deep Archive.
Option_D: Move the catalog to an Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) file system.

Question: A company stores call transcript files on a monthly basis. Users access the files randomly within 1 year of the call, but users access the files infrequently after 1 year. The company wants to optimize its solution by giving users the ability to query and retrieve files that are less than 1-year-old as quickly as possible. A delay in retrieving older files is acceptable. Which solution will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?
Option_A: Store individual files with tags in Amazon S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval. Query the tags to retrieve the files from S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval.
Option_B: Store individual files in Amazon S3 Intelligent-Tiering. Use S3 Lifecycle policies to move the files to S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval after 1 year. Query and retrieve the files that are in Amazon S3 by using Amazon Athena.
Option_C: Store individual files with tags in Amazon S3 Standard storage. Store search metadata for each archive in Amazon S3 Standard storage. Use S3 Lifecycle policies to move the files to S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval after 1 year.
Option_D: Store individual files in Amazon S3 Standard storage. Use S3 Lifecycle policies to move the files to S3 Glacier Deep Archive after 1 year. Store search metadata in Amazon RDS.

Question: A company has a production workload that runs on 1,000 Amazon EC2 Linux instances. The workload is powered by third-party software. The company needs to patch the third-party software on all EC2 instances as quickly as possible to remediate a critical security vulnerability. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?
Option_A: Create an AWS Lambda function to apply the patch to all EC2 instances.
Option_B: Configure AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager to apply the patch to all EC2 instances.
Option_C: Schedule an AWS Systems Manager maintenance window to apply the patch to all EC2 instances.
Option_D: Use AWS Systems Manager Run Command to run a custom command that applies the patch to all EC2 instances.

Question: A company is developing an application that provides order shipping statistics for retrieval by a REST API. The company wants to extract the shipping statistics, organize the data into an easy-to-read HTML format, and send the report to several email addresses at the same time every morning. Which combination of steps should a solutions architect take to meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
Option_A: Configure the application to send the data to Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose.
Option_B: Use Amazon Simple Email Service (Amazon SES) to format the data and to send the report by email.
Option_C: Create an Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) scheduled event that invokes an AWS Glue job to query the application's API for the data.
Option_D: Create an Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) scheduled event that invokes an AWS Lambda function to query the application's API for the data.
Option_E: Store the application data in Amazon S3. Create an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) Topics as an S3 event destination to send the report by email.

Question: A company wants to migrate its on-premises application to AWS. The application produces output files that vary in size from tens of gigabytes to hundreds of terabytes. The application data must be stored in a standard file system structure. The company wants a solution that scales automatically, is highly available, and requires minimum operational overhead. Which solution will meet these requirements?
Option_A: Migrate the application to run as containers on Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS). Use Amazon S3 for storage.
Option_B: Migrate the application to run as containers on Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS). Use Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) for storage.
Option_C: Migrate the application to Amazon EC2 instances in a Multi-AZ Auto Scaling group. Use Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) for storage.
Option_D: Migrate the application to Amazon EC2 instances in a Multi-AZ Auto Scaling group. Use Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) for storage.

Question: A company needs to store its accounting records in Amazon S3. The records must be immediately accessible for 1 year and then must be archived for an additional 9 years. No one at the company, including administrative users and root users, can be able to delete the records during the entire 10-year period. The records must be stored with maximum resiliency. Which solution will meet these requirements?
Option_A: Store the records in S3 Glacier for the entire 10-year period. Use an access control policy to deny deletion of the records for a period of 10 years.
Option_B: Store the records by using S3 Intelligent-Tiering. Use an IAM policy to deny deletion of the records. After 10 years, change the IAM policy to allow deletion.
Option_C: Use an S3 Lifecycle policy to transition the records from S3 Standard to S3 Glacier Deep Archive after 1 year. Use S3 Object Lock in compliance mode for a period of 10 years.
Option_D: Use an S3 Lifecycle policy to transition the records from S3 Standard to S3 One Zone-Infrequent Access (S3 One Zone-IA) after 1 year. Use S3 Object Lock in governance mode.
Topics: S3, S3 Glacier, S3 Object Lock

Question: A company runs multiple Windows workloads on AWS. The company's employees use Windows file shares that are hosted on two Amazon EC2 instances. The file shares synchronize data between themselves and maintain duplicate copies. The company wants a highly available and durable storage solution that preserves how users currently access the files. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?
Option_A: Migrate all the data to Amazon S3. Set up IAM authentication for users to access files.
Option_B: Set up an Amazon S3 File Gateway. Mount the S3 File Gateway on the existing EC2 instances.
Option_C: Extend the file share environment to Amazon FSx for Windows File Server with a Multi-AZ configuration. Migrate all the data to FSx for Windows File Server.
Option_D: Extend the file share environment to Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) with a Multi-AZ configuration.

Question: A solutions architect is designing a VPC architecture that includes multiple subnets. The architecture will host applications that use Amazon EC2 instances and Amazon RDS DB instances. The architecture consists of six subnets in two Availability Zones. Each Availability Zone includes a public subnet, a private subnet, and a dedicated subnet for databases. Only EC2 instances that run in the private subnets can have access to the RDS databases. Which solution will meet these requirements?
Option_A: Create a new route table that excludes the route to the public subnets' CIDR blocks. Associate the route table with the database subnets.
Option_B: Create a security group that denies inbound traffic from the security group that is assigned to instances in the public subnets. Attach the security group to the DB instances.
Option_C: Create a security group that allows inbound traffic from the security group that is assigned to instances in the private subnets. Attach the security group to the DB instances.
Option_D: Create a new peering connection between the public subnets and the private subnets.

Question: A company has registered its domain name with Amazon Route 53. The company uses Amazon API Gateway in the ca-central-1 Region as a public interface for its backend microservice APIs. Third-party services consume the APIs securely. The company wants to design its API Gateway URL with the company's domain name and corresponding certificate so that the third-party services can use HTTPS. Which solution will meet these requirements?
Option_A: Create stage variables in API Gateway with Name="Endpoint-URL" and Value="Company Domain Name" to overwrite the default URL. Import the public certificate into ACM.
Option_B: Create Route 53 DNS records with the company's domain name. Point the alias record to the Regional API Gateway stage endpoint. Import the certificate into ACM in us-east-1.
Option_C: Create a Regional API Gateway endpoint. Associate the API Gateway endpoint with the company's domain name. Import the certificate into ACM in the same Region. Attach it to the endpoint.
Option_D: Create a Regional API Gateway endpoint. Associate the API Gateway endpoint with the company's domain name. Import the certificate into ACM in us-east-1. Attach it to the APIs.

Question: A company is running a popular social media website. The website gives users the ability to upload images to share with other users. The company wants to make sure that the images do not contain inappropriate content. The company needs a solution that minimizes development effort. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?
Option_A: Use Amazon Comprehend to detect inappropriate content. Use human review for low-confidence predictions.
Option_B: Use Amazon Rekognition to detect inappropriate content. Use human review for low-confidence predictions.
Option_C: Use Amazon SageMaker to detect inappropriate content. Use ground truth to label low-confidence predictions.
Option_D: Use AWS Fargate to deploy a custom machine learning model to detect inappropriate content.

Question: A company wants to run its critical applications in containers to meet requirements for scalability and availability. The company prefers to focus on maintenance of the critical applications. The company does not want to be responsible for provisioning and managing the underlying infrastructure that runs the containerized workload. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?
Option_A: Use Amazon EC2 instances, and install Docker on the instances.
Option_B: Use Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) on Amazon EC2 worker nodes.
Option_C: Use Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) on AWS Fargate.
Option_D: Use Amazon EC2 instances from an Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS)-optimized Amazon Machine Image (AMI).

Question: A company hosts more than 300 global websites and applications. The company requires a platform to analyze more than 30 TB of clickstream data each day. What should a solutions architect do to transmit and process the clickstream data?
Option_A: Design an AWS Data Pipeline to archive the data to an Amazon S3 bucket and run an Amazon EMR cluster with the data to generate analytics.
Option_B: Create an Auto Scaling group of Amazon EC2 instances to process the data and send it to an Amazon S3 data lake for Amazon Redshift to use for analysis.
Option_C: Cache the data to Amazon CloudFront. Store the data in an Amazon S3 bucket. When an object is added to the S3 bucket, run an AWS Lambda function to process the data.
Option_D: Collect the data from Amazon Kinesis Data Streams. Use Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose to transmit the data to an Amazon S3 data lake. Load the data in Amazon Redshift.

Question: A company's HTTP application is behind a Network Load Balancer (NLB). The NLB's target group is configured to use an Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling group with multiple EC2 instances that run the web service. The company notices that the NLB is not detecting HTTP errors for the application. These errors require a manual restart of the EC2 instances. The company needs to improve the application's availability without writing custom scripts or code. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?
Option_A: Enable HTTP health checks on the NLB, supplying the URL of the company's application.
Option_B: Add a cron job to the EC2 instances to check the local application's logs once each minute. If HTTP errors are detected, the application will restart.
Option_C: Replace the NLB with an Application Load Balancer. Enable HTTP health checks by supplying the URL of the company's application. Configure an Auto Scaling action to replace unhealthy instances.
Option_D: Create an Amazon Cloud Watch alarm that monitors the Unhealthy HostCount metric for the NLB.

Question: A company runs a shopping application that uses Amazon DynamoDB to store customer information. In case of data corruption, a solutions architect needs to design a solution that meets a recovery point objective (RPO) of 15 minutes and a recovery time objective (RTO) of 1 hour. What should the solutions architect recommend to meet these requirements?
Option_A: Configure DynamoDB global tables. For RPO recovery, point the application to a different AWS Region.
Option_B: Configure DynamoDB point-in-time recovery. For RPO recovery, restore to the desired point in time.
Option_C: Export the DynamoDB data to Amazon S3 Glacier on a daily basis. For RPO recovery, import the data from S3 Glacier to DynamoDB.
Option_D: Schedule Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) snapshots for the DynamoDB table every 15 minutes.

Question: A company runs a photo processing application that needs to frequently upload and download pictures from Amazon S3 buckets that are located in the same AWS Region. A solutions architect has noticed an increased cost in data transfer fees and needs to implement a solution to reduce these costs. How can the solutions architect meet this requirement?
Option_A: Deploy Amazon API Gateway into a public subnet and adjust the route table to route S3 calls through it.
Option_B: Deploy a NAT gateway into a public subnet and attach an endpoint policy that allows access to the S3 buckets.
Option_C: Deploy the application into a public subnet and allow it to route through an internet gateway to access the S3 buckets.
Option_D: Deploy an S3 VPC gateway endpoint into the VPC and attach an endpoint policy that allows access to the S3 buckets.

Question: A company recently launched Linux-based application instances on Amazon EC2 in a private subnet and launched a Linux-based bastion host on an Amazon EC2 instance in a public subnet of a VPC. A solutions architect needs to connect from the on-premises network, through the company's internet connection, to the bastion host, and to the application servers. The solutions architect must make sure that the security groups of all the EC2 instances will allow that access. Which combination of steps should the solutions architect take to meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
Option_A: Replace the current security group of the bastion host with one that only allows inbound access from the application instances.
Option_B: Replace the current security group of the bastion host with one that only allows inbound access from the internal IP range for the company.
Option_C: Replace the current security group of the bastion host with one that only allows inbound access from the external IP range for the company.
Option_D: Replace the current security group of the application instances with one that allows inbound SSH access from only the private IP address of the bastion host.
Option_E: Replace the current security group of the application instances with one that allows inbound SSH access from only the public IP address of the bastion host.

Question: A company wants to allow its users to upload images in an application that is hosted in the AWS Cloud. The company needs a solution that automatically resizes the images so that the images can be displayed on multiple device types. The application experiences unpredictable traffic patterns throughout the day. The company is seeking a highly available solution that maximizes scalability. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?
Option_A: Create a static website hosted in Amazon S3 that invokes AWS Lambda functions to resize the images and store the images in an Amazon S3 bucket.
Option_B: Create a static website hosted in Amazon CloudFront that invokes AWS Step Functions to resize the images and store the images in an Amazon RDS database.
Option_C: Create a dynamic website hosted on a web server that runs on an Amazon EC2 instance. Configure a process that runs on the EC2 instance to resize the images.
Option_D: Create a dynamic website hosted on a serverless compute platform.

Question: A company needs to give a globally distributed development team secure access to the company's AWS resources in a way that complies with security policies. The company currently uses an on-premises Active Directory for internal authentication. The company uses AWS Organizations to manage multiple AWS accounts that support multiple projects. The company needs a solution to integrate with the existing infrastructure to provide centralized identity management and access control. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
Option_A: Set up AWS Directory Service to create an AWS managed Microsoft AD and establish a trust relationship.
Option_B: Set up an Amazon Cognito identity pool. Configure AWS IAM Identity Center (AWS Single Sign-On) to accept Amazon Cognito authentication.
Option_C: Create individual IAM users for each member in all the teams with role-based permissions.
Option_D: Use AWS Organizations to create separate accounts for each team. Implement cross-account IAM roles.
Topics: AWS IAM Identity Center, AWS Directory Service, AWS Organizations

Question: A company has migrated a fleet of hundreds of on-premises virtual machines (VMs) to Amazon EC2 instances. The instances run a diverse fleet of Windows Server versions along with several Linux distributions. The company wants a solution that will automate inventory and updates of the operating systems. The company also needs a summary of common vulnerabilities of each instance for regular monthly reviews. What should a solutions architect recommend to meet these requirements?
Option_A: Set up AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager to manage all the EC2 instances. Configure AWS Security Hub to produce monthly reports.
Option_B: Set up AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager to manage all the EC2 instances. Deploy Amazon Inspector, and configure monthly reports.
Option_C: Set up AWS Shield Advanced, and configure monthly reports. Deploy AWS Config to automate patch installations on the EC2 instances.
Option_D: Set up Amazon GuardDuty in the account to monitor all EC2 instances. Deploy AWS Config to automate patch installations.

Question: A company hosts its application in the AWS Cloud. The application runs on Amazon EC2 instances in an Auto Scaling group behind an Elastic Load Balancing (ELB) load balancer. The application connects to an Amazon DynamoDB table. For disaster recovery (DR) purposes, the company wants to ensure that the application is available from another AWS Region with minimal downtime. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST downtime?
Option_A: Create an Auto Scaling group and an ELB in the DR Region. Configure the DynamoDB table as a global table. Configure DNS failover to point to the new DR Region's ELB.
Option_B: Create an AWS CloudFormation template to create EC2 instances, ELBs, and DynamoDB tables to be launched when necessary. Configure DNS failover.
Option_C: Create an AWS CloudFormation template to create EC2 instances and an ELB to be launched when necessary. Configure the DynamoDB table as a global table.
Option_D: Create manual snapshots of the application and replicate them to the DR region monthly.
Topics: Amazon DynamoDB, Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling, Elastic Load Balancing

Question: A company runs an application on Amazon EC2 instances in a private subnet. The application needs to store and retrieve data in Amazon S3 buckets. According to regulatory requirements, the data must not travel across the public internet. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?
Option_A: Deploy a NAT gateway to access the S3 buckets.
Option_B: Deploy AWS Storage Gateway to access the S3 buckets.
Option_C: Deploy an S3 interface endpoint to access the S3 buckets.
Option_D: Deploy an S3 gateway endpoint to access the S3 buckets.

Question: A company wants to ensure that its application is highly available. The company wants to distribute incoming traffic across multiple instances at the network layer. Which combination of steps will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively? (Choose two.)
Option_A: Configure new EC2 instances in a different Availability Zone. Use Amazon Route 53 to route traffic to all instances.
Option_B: Configure a Network Load Balancer in front of the EC2 instances.
Option_C: Configure a Network Load Balancer for TCP traffic to the instances. Configure an Application Load Balancer for HTTP and HTTPS traffic to the instances.
Option_D: Create an Auto Scaling group for the EC2 instances. Configure the Auto Scaling group to use multiple Availability Zones. Configure the Auto Scaling group to run application health checks.
Option_E: Create an Amazon CloudWatch alarm. Configure the alarm to restart EC2 instances that transition to a stopped state.
Topics: Amazon EC2, Network Load Balancer, Auto Scaling

Question: A company has an application that places hundreds of .csv files into an Amazon S3 bucket every hour. The files are 1 GB in size. Each time a file is uploaded, the company needs to convert the file to Apache Parquet format and place the output file into an S3 bucket. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
Option_A: Create an AWS Lambda function to download the .csv files, convert the files to Parquet format, and place the output files in an S3 bucket. Invoke the Lambda function for each S3 PUT event.
Option_B: Create an Apache Spark job to read the .csv files, convert the files to Parquet format, and place the output files in an S3 bucket. Create an AWS Lambda function to invoke the Spark job.
Option_C: Create an AWS Glue table and an AWS Glue crawler for the S3 bucket. Schedule an AWS Lambda function to periodically use Amazon Athena to query the AWS Glue table.
Option_D: Create an AWS Glue table and an AWS Glue crawler for the S3 bucket. Create an AWS Glue ETL job that is triggered by each S3 PUT event. Configure the ETL job to convert the .csv files to Parquet format.

Question: A company is implementing new data retention policies for all databases that run on Amazon RDS DB instances. The company must retain daily backups for a minimum period of 2 years. The backups must be consistent and restorable. Which solution should a solutions architect recommend to meet these requirements?
Option_A: Create a backup vault in AWS Backup to retain RDS backups. Create a new backup plan with a daily schedule and an expiration period of 2 years after creation. Assign the RDS DB instances to the backup plan.
Option_B: Configure a backup window for the RDS DB instances for daily snapshots. Assign a snapshot retention policy of 2 years to each RDS DB instance. Use Amazon Data Lifecycle Manager (Amazon DLM).
Option_C: Configure database transaction logs to be automatically backed up to Amazon CloudWatch Logs. Set the log group retention policy to 2 years.
Option_D: Use AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) change data capture (CDC) to create daily backups of the RDS DB instances in Amazon S3. Configure an S3 Lifecycle policy to delete the backups after 2 years.

Question: A company maintains an application that runs on Amazon EC2 instances. The application has several backend services, including an Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL database and an Amazon OpenSearch Service cluster. The company's security team requires that all network traffic to these backend services be encrypted in transit. The company also wants to simplify how it manages and rotates the SSL/TLS certificates that the services use. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
Option_A: Use AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) to generate certificates for each service. Configure each service to use the appropriate certificate to encrypt traffic.
Option_B: Use AWS Certificate Manager Private Certificate Authority (ACM PCA) to create a private CA. Generate certificates from the private CA for each service.
Option_C: Generate self-signed certificates for each service. Configure each service to use the appropriate certificate to encrypt traffic.
Option_D: Use third-party certificates for each service. Configure each service to use the appropriate certificate to encrypt traffic.

Question: A company has an on-premises Active Directory and wants to give its users the ability to access Amazon S3. The company has 1,500 users, and wants to avoid creating a new IAM user for each user. Which solution will meet these requirements?
Option_A: Federate the on-premises Active Directory with IAM by using Active Directory Federation Services (ADFS).
Option_B: Use a directory utility to create IAM users from the users in the on-premises Active Directory.
Option_C: Create a new IAM user for each user. Create a one-to-one mapping between the on-premises Active Directory and the IAM users.
Option_D: Use AWS Directory Service to create a new directory that synchronizes with the on-premises Active Directory.

Question: A company wants to serve its new static website from an Amazon S3 bucket. The company needs the website to load as quickly as possible for users around the world. The company also needs the website's traffic to be encrypted. Which solution will meet these requirements?
Option_A: Configure an S3 bucket for website hosting. Configure an Amazon Route 53 record to point to the S3 bucket.
Option_B: Configure an S3 bucket for website hosting. Configure an Amazon CloudFront distribution that has the S3 bucket as its origin. Configure a DNS record to point to the CloudFront distribution.
Option_C: Configure an S3 bucket for website hosting. Configure S3 Cross-Region Replication to buckets in all other AWS Regions. Configure an Amazon Route 53 record.
Option_D: Configure an S3 bucket for website hosting. Enable S3 Transfer Acceleration on the S3 bucket. Configure an Amazon Route 53 record to point to the S3 bucket.

Question: A company hosts its enterprise resource planning (ERP) system in the us-east-1 Region. The system runs on Amazon EC2 instances. Customers use a public API that is hosted on the EC2 instances to exchange information with the ERP system. International customers report high latency when they use the public API. The company wants to improve the performance of its public API for international customers. The company also wants a solution that requires the least possible effort to implement. Which solution will meet these requirements?
Option_A: Use Amazon CloudFront to cache the public API responses.
Option_B: Use AWS Direct Connect to set up dedicated network connections for each international customer.
Option_C: Use AWS Global Accelerator to improve the availability and performance of the public API.
Option_D: Use AWS Site-to-Site VPN to set up secure connections between each international customer and the ERP system.

Question: A solutions architect has an Amazon S3 bucket that contains more than 100,000 objects. The S3 bucket has S3 Versioning enabled. The company has a new requirement that every object in the S3 bucket be retained for a minimum of 1 year. No users can be able to modify or delete any files in the S3 repository during this time. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
Option_A: Enable MFA Delete on the S3 bucket. Set up an S3 Lifecycle policy to enforce a minimum retention period of 365 days for every object.
Option_B: Configure S3 Cross-Region Replication to a new S3 bucket in another AWS Region. Configure an S3 Lifecycle policy to enforce a minimum retention period of 365 days.
Option_C: Create an S3 Glacier vault. Create a vault lock policy that sets a minimum retention period of 365 days for every object.
Option_D: Enable S3 Object Lock on the S3 bucket. Set a default retention period of 365 days in compliance mode.

Question: A company is hosting a static website on Amazon S3. The website needs to be available to a global audience with low latency. A solutions architect created an Amazon CloudFront distribution and configured the S3 bucket as the origin. During a security review, the solutions architect discovers that users can bypass CloudFront and access the files directly from the S3 bucket. What should the solutions architect do to secure the S3 bucket?
Option_A: Create a security group. Assign the security group to the CloudFront distribution. Configure the S3 bucket permissions so that only the security group has read permission.
Option_B: Create an IAM role. Assign the IAM role to the CloudFront distribution. Configure the S3 bucket permissions so that only the IAM role has read permission.
Option_C: Create a network ACL. Assign the network ACL to the CloudFront distribution. Configure the S3 bucket permissions so that only the network ACL has read permission.
Option_D: Create an origin access identity (OAI). Assign the OAI to the CloudFront distribution. Configure the S3 bucket permissions so that only the OAI has read permission.

Question: A company wants to provide historical performance reports for its sales team. These reports will be hosted on AWS. The reports will be accessed globally by a limited number of users. The reports must be available for download with the fastest possible response time. The solution must require limited provisioning and configuration. Which solution meets these requirements MOST cost-effectively?
Option_A: Amazon CloudFront and Amazon S3
Option_B: AWS Lambda and Amazon DynamoDB
Option_C: Application Load Balancer with Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling
Option_D: Amazon Route 53 with internal Application Load Balancers

Question: A company is migrating an on-premises Oracle database to Amazon RDS for Oracle. The database is 15 TB in size. The company requires a disaster recovery (DR) solution with an RPO of less than 10 minutes and an RTO of less than 4 hours. The DR solution must be in a different AWS Region from the primary database. Which DR solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
Option_A: Migrate the Oracle database to Amazon RDS for Oracle. Configure a cross-Region automated backup.
Option_B: Migrate the Oracle database to Amazon RDS for Oracle. Take database snapshots, and copy the snapshots to another AWS Region.
Option_C: Migrate the Oracle database to Amazon RDS Custom for Oracle. Create a read replica for the database in another AWS Region.
Option_D: Migrate the Oracle database to Amazon RDS for Oracle. Create a standby database in another Availability Zone.

Question: A company needs a serverless solution that can analyze data in Amazon S3 by using SQL. The data must be encrypted at rest in Amazon S3. The data must be replicated to another AWS Region. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
Option_A: Configure S3 Cross-Region Replication (CRR) on the S3 bucket. Use server-side encryption with AWS KMS multi-Region keys (SSE-KMS). Use Amazon Athena to query the data.
Option_B: Configure S3 Cross-Region Replication (CRR) on the S3 bucket. Use server-side encryption with Amazon S3 managed keys (SSE-S3). Use Amazon Redshift to query the data.
Option_C: Create a second S3 bucket in another Region. Create an AWS Lambda function to copy new objects from the source S3 bucket to the second S3 bucket. Use Amazon RDS.
Option_D: Create a second S3 bucket in another Region. Create an AWS Lambda function to copy new objects. Use server-side encryption with Amazon S3 managed keys (SSE-S3). Use Athena.

Question: A company wants to consume a third-party software as a service (SaaS) offering. The provider has made the service available in its own VPC on AWS. The company must be able to initiate a connection to the service privately and without restriction. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
Option_A: Ask the provider to create a VPC peering connection.
Option_B: Ask the provider to create a NAT gateway in its VPC.
Option_C: Ask the provider to create an internet gateway in its VPC.
Option_D: Ask the provider to create a VPC endpoint for the target service. Use AWS PrivateLink to connect.

Question: A company is performing a zero-downtime migration of its on-premises PostgreSQL database to an Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL DB cluster. A solutions architect must implement a solution to ensure that the data in the source database and the target database are synchronized throughout the migration process. Which combination of actions should the solutions architect take to meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
Option_A: Use AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) with ongoing replication.
Option_B: Create a database backup of the on-premises database.
Option_C: Create an AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) replication server.
Option_D: Create an Amazon S3 bucket to stage the initial data load.
Option_E: Create an AWS Snowball Edge device to ship the on-premises data to AWS.

Question: A company uses AWS Organizations to manage multiple AWS accounts. A security team requires that all notifications sent to the root user email address must not be missed and must be limited to account administrators only. Which solution meets these requirements with the LEAST implementation effort?
Option_A: Configure Amazon Simple Email Service (Amazon SES) to receive all root user email notifications. Integrate Amazon SES with an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue.
Option_B: Configure the root user email address as a distribution list that all account administrators can access. In the Organizations management account, configure alternate contacts.
Option_C: Create an IAM role that has the required permissions to send email notifications to the account administrators. Configure an Amazon EventBridge rule that initiates an AWS Lambda function.
Option_D: Configure the root user email address of each account to forward to a centralized email address. Configure an Amazon EventBridge rule that initiates an AWS Lambda function.

Question: A company has an application that uses a RabbitMQ queue and a PostgreSQL database. The application runs on a single Amazon EC2 instance. A solutions architect needs to rearchitect this application to be highly available with minimal operational overhead. Which solution will meet these requirements?
Option_A: Migrate the RabbitMQ queue to an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) standard queue. Migrate the PostgreSQL database to a Multi-AZ deployment of Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL.
Option_B: Migrate the RabbitMQ queue to a redundant pair of brokers on Amazon MQ. Migrate the PostgreSQL database to a Multi-AZ deployment of Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL.
Option_C: Migrate the RabbitMQ queue to an Amazon Kinesis data stream. Migrate the PostgreSQL database to Amazon DynamoDB with provisioned capacity.
Option_D: Migrate the RabbitMQ queue to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) Topics. Migrate the PostgreSQL database to a Multi-AZ deployment of Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL.

Question: A company has an analytics application that generates reports from data that is stored in an Amazon S3 bucket. After the reports are generated, the analytics application copies the reports to a destination S3 bucket in a different AWS Region. The company wants to implement an event-driven solution to meet new requirements. The new requirements are to start an AWS Lambda function for pattern matching and to start an AWS Step Functions state machine with an AWS Glue job for analysis. These actions must be triggered as soon as a new report is available in the destination S3 bucket. Which solution will meet these requirements?
Option_A: Set up S3 Event Notifications in the destination S3 bucket to send notifications directly to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) Topics.
Option_B: Set up S3 Event Notifications in the destination S3 bucket to send notifications directly to an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue.
Option_C: Set up S3 Event Notifications in the destination S3 bucket to directly invoke the Lambda function and the Step Functions state machine.
Option_D: Set up S3 Event Notifications in the destination S3 bucket to send notifications to Amazon EventBridge. Configure an EventBridge rule that has the Lambda function and the Step Functions state machine as targets.

Question: A company runs a web application with three different environments: development, testing, and production. The architecture consists of a data ingestion layer, an application front end, and an API layer. The data ingestion layer runs on Amazon EC2 and can tolerate interruptions. The application front end runs on AWS Fargate. The API layer runs on AWS Lambda. The company has noticed that the usage is predictable for the next year. A solutions architect needs to provide a purchasing strategy to minimize the cost of the architecture. Which combination of actions should the solutions architect take to meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
Option_A: Use Spot Instances for the data ingestion layer.
Option_B: Use On-Demand Instances for the data ingestion layer.
Option_C: Purchase a 1-year Compute Savings Plan for the front end and API layer.
Option_D: Purchase a 1-year EC2 Instance Savings Plan for the front end and API layer.
Option_E: Purchase Reserved Instances for the data ingestion layer.

Question: A company hosts a web portal that provides users with personalized views of their data. The website runs on Amazon EC2 instances behind an Application Load Balancer (ALB). The EC2 instances are in an Auto Scaling group. The website uses an Amazon S3 bucket for static content. The company wants to improve the performance of its portal for its global user base. What should a solutions architect do to meet this requirement?

Option_B: Replicate the architecture in a second AWS Region. Use latency-based routing to serve content from the closest Region.
Option_C: Use Amazon CloudFront to serve only the static content.
Option_D: Use an Amazon Route 53 geolocation routing policy to serve content from the closest Region.

Question: A gaming company hosts a multiplayer game that uses the UDP protocol for communication between clients and servers. The company's servers are running on Amazon EC2 instances in an Auto Scaling group. The company needs a highly available load balancing solution for the game servers. The solution must provide the company with full control over the underlying operating system because the application runs on a modified Linux kernel. Which solution will meet these requirements?

Option_B: Create an Amazon CloudFront distribution that forwards traffic to a Network Load Balancer. Use a custom Amazon Machine Image (AMI).
Option_C: Create a Network Load Balancer with a UDP listener. Use a custom Amazon Machine Image (AMI) to configure the Auto Scaling group.
Option_D: Create an Amazon API Gateway API with a UDP listener that forwards traffic to an Application Load Balancer. Use a custom AMI.
